zh.lifeSummary: 當Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg於大約1185年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀時，他的父親Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige32歲，母親Agnes von Zähringen26歲。他和Adelheid von Neuffen至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。他於1230年被列為regentschaft als graf von freiburg。他去世於1236年6月25日在德國巴登可萊斯 埃門丁根埃門丁根埃門丁根享年52歲，埋葬在德國巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根埃門丁根。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg于大约1185年出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀时，他的父亲Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige32岁，母亲Agnes von Zähringen26岁。他和Adelheid von Neuffen至少有5个儿子和1个女儿。他于1230年被列为regentschaft als graf von freiburg。他去世于1236年6月25日在德国巴登可萊斯 埃門丁根埃門丁根埃門丁根享年52岁，埋葬在德国巴登-爾特波格可萊斯 埃門丁根埃門丁根。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Urach,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Cheskel, Moshe, Chaim, Mendel, Pinchas, Bernat, Hershel, Meyer, Moche, Mort, Rivka, Rivky.

German: topographic name from Middle High German brache ‘fallow land, pastureland’, originally ‘newly plowed land’. Alternatively, a habitational name from any of the places called Braach (Hesse) or Brach (Baden-Württemberg, Franconia).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Barach  .

variants.name: Breach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal.
English (Sussex): variant of   Brach  .

variants.name: Brecher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Chaim, Yosef, Baila, Chana, Ephraim, Herschel, Hershel, Meir, Meyer, Pnina, Shimon.  German Armin, Oskar, Bernd, Erwin, Gerhard.
English: variant of Brach 2, from Middle English breche, Old English brǣc, brēc ‘newly cultivated land’, the -er suffix denoting an inhabitant.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from an agent derivative of German brechen ‘to break’, an occupational name for someone who crushed hemp or flax, or a nickname for a law breaker or evildoer.

variants.name: Bracher
meanings: English: from an agent derivative of Middle English brech ‘land broken up for cultivation’ (Old English brǣc, brēc), denoting a dweller by newly cultivated land. Compare Brach 2 and Breach .
Swiss German: variant of German Brachmann (see Brachman ).
German: variant of   Brach   1.

variants.name: Brachman
meanings: Americanized form of German Brachmann: variant of Brach , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Brech
meanings: German: variant of   Brach   1 or   Brecht  .
English: variant of   Breach   (see   Brach   2).

variants.name: Barach
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Serbian Barać and Barač (see Barac ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): acronymic surname from the first letters of the Hebrew patronymic phrase Ben Rabi Hayyim ‘son of rabbi Hayyim’.

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Junichi, Yoshihiro, Haruo, Hideo, Hiroaki, Kaori, Katsumi, Kazuhide, Kazuhiko, Koichi, Maki, Masae.
Japanese: written 荻野 ‘reed plain’, of which Japan has many. It is found mostly in eastern Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Freiburg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Freiburg or Freiberg .
Americanized form of Vreeburg, shortened variant of Vredenburg .

variants.name: Freiberg
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Freiberg, from Middle High German vrī ‘free, independent’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’ (possibly an altered form of burg ‘castle, fortified town’).

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the many given names ending in -gino, e.g. Giorgino, pet form of Giorgio , and Luigino, pet form of Luigi .

en.lifeSummary: When Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg was born about 1185, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige, was 32 and his mother, Agnes von Zähringen, was 26. He had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with Adelheid von Neuffen. In 1230, his occupation is listed as regentschaft als graf von freiburg. He died on 25 June 1236, in Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Baden, Germany, at the age of 52, and was buried in Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

fr.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Allemagne
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg est né environ 1185, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Zähringen, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille avec Adelheid von Neuffen. En 1230, sa profession de son indiquée est regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Il est décédé le 25 juin 1236, à Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Allemagne, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg um 1185 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige 32 und seine Mutter Agnes von Zähringen 26. Er hatte mit Adelheid von Neuffen mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1230 ist sein Beruf als regentschaft als graf von freiburg aufgeführt. Er starb am 25. Juni 1236 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Deutschland und wurde in Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg è nato il circa 1185 nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Agnes von Zähringen, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia con Adelheid von Neuffen. Nel 1230, la sua occupazione è indicata come regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Morì il 25 giugno 1236, nel Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Germania, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburgが約1185年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチで生まれた時，父Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtigeは32歳，母Agnes von Zähringenは26歳であった。彼はAdelheid von Neuffenとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。1230年，職業はregentschaft als graf von freiburgと記録されている。彼は1236年6月25日にドイツバデンクレイス エンメンディンジェンエンメンディンジェンエンメンディンジェンで52歳で死去し，ドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグクレイス エンメンディンジェンエンメンディンジェンにて埋葬された。

ko.Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendin: 독일 바든 크레이스 에멘딩 에멘딩 에멘딩
ko.lifeSummary: Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg(이)가 대략 1185년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes von Zähringen(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Adelheid von Neuffen(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1230년, 그의 직업은 regentschaft als graf von freiburg이었다. 그는 1236년 6월 25일 독일 바든 크레이스 에멘딩 에멘딩 에멘딩에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바데-wu르테버그 크레이스 에멘딩 에멘딩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg nasceu aproximadamente 1185, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Zähringen, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha com Adelheid von Neuffen. Em 1230, sua profissão aparece como regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Ele faleceu em 25 de junho de 1236, em Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Alemanha, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg родился около 1185 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Zähringen, 26. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь от Adelheid von Neuffen. В 1230 году он работал по профессии regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Он умер 25 июня 1236 г. в местности эмменденген, эмменденген, крис эмменденген, баден, Германия в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности эмменденген, крис эмменденген, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Egino V. Graf von Urach, Egino I. Graf von Freiburg nació aproximadamente 1185, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Egino IV. Graf von Urach genannt der Bärtige, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Agnes von Zähringen, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija con Adelheid von Neuffen. En 1230, su ocupación registrada era regentschaft als graf von freiburg. Murió el 25 de junio de 1236, en Emmendingen, Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden, Alemania, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Emmendingen, Kreis Emmendingen, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania.
